text.title: Agency-to-SaaS Readiness Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Agency-to-SaaS Readiness Checklist plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: Before the SaaS pivot, the agency MUST hold unambiguous IP rights to all candidate-product code; client-funded codebases without explicit licence-back MUST be excluded.
rule.rationale: IP claims surface during diligence and tank valuations; clearing IP before pivot is cheaper than litigating during a deal.

rule.statement: The candidate SaaS MUST come from an asset already used on 3+ paying engagements; one-off code MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: One-off code lacks the production hardening + customer evidence that justifies SaaS pricing; the 3+ rule is the minimum proof of repeatability.

rule.statement: The agency retainer base MUST cover 18+ months of SaaS-pivot operating cost; pivots funded by debt or runway-burn MUST be flagged red.

rationale.source: Jason Cohen — A Smart Bear blog

rule.rationale: SaaS pivots that hit the wall at month 9 force founders back into agency work; the 18-month rule is the realistic floor.

rule.statement: The founder MUST commit ≥50% of weekly hours to the SaaS work; <50% commitments MUST be flagged as 'side project' not 'pivot'.
rule.rationale: Sub-50% founder time produces a side-project velocity that loses to even modest competition; the 50% rule separates pivots from hobbies.

rule.statement: Every readiness row MUST have a named owner + due date; orphan rows MUST be deleted or assigned.
rule.rationale: Orphan readiness rows create false confidence; the named-owner rule is the only structural gate against checklist theatre.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
